issue_title: Group search of nested groups does not work as expected
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The issue described in [#34229](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/34229) is still present in Keycloak 26.1.3.

The issue occurs specifically in the user profile when searching for a subgroup within a nested group structure. Instead of returning the exact subgroup being searched for, the result still displays the parent group, making it difficult to manage and select deeply nested groups.


### Version

26.1.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When searching for "Target_Subgroup", the result should return only "Target_Subgroup" and not its parent group.

### Actual behavior

If I search for "Target_Subgroup" in the user profile, the result still displays "Top_Group" instead of "Target_Subgroup."

### How to Reproduce?

For example, given the following structure:
    Top_Group
        Target_Subgroup

1. Go to Users
2. Select a user
3. Go to Groups Tab
4. Click 'Join Group'
5. Type search term matching one of the nested groups
6. Top level group instead of a subgroup is returned

The same behavior occurs with the “add user / join group” function.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: "remember me" session are reset as standard session after browser restart
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When a login flow requires a user interaction on a valid "Remember me" session.
After a browser restart, this login flow transform the "Remember me" session as a regular session.
Then, this session is lost after a second browser restart

### Version

26.0.7 (the bug is present on 26.1.2, the 26.1.3 is not tested but it must be present)

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

On a realm with the feature "remember me" enabled on the login screen.
When a user authenticate and check the "remember me" checkbox on the login screen, the user to remain logged in between browser restarts until session expires.

This behaviour works on the stepup flow (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_step-up-flow) and, after a browser restart, the user is challenged only for the second authentication factor (it does not need to provide the passsword).

### Actual behavior

After a browser restart, a "remember me" session become a standard session when a login flow requiring a user interaction is triggered.
Thus after a second  browser restart, the user is logged out.


### How to Reproduce?

In our project, we use some custom authenticator but this behaviour can be reproduce with the standard authenticator provided with keycloak and the stepup flow (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_step-up-flow) that can trigger a user interaction when logging in to a client application

1 - activate the "remember me" feature on the master realm 
2 - configure "long" session for "remember me" : 40 days for idle on SSO Remember me

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3601c324-7f81-4c40-b6aa-9538f909c796)

3 - Configure the stepup flow (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_step-up-flow) the TOTP step has a mac-age of 0 as defined in the documentation and it allows to show the bug more easily

4 - Configure a user with a totp credential
5 - Login to a client application with the "remember me" checkbox
==> here some persistent cookies are defined with a long max-age :

- KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY
- KEYCLOAK_SESSION
- KEYCLOAK_REMEMBER_ME

The AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie is a session cookie

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8524f1fe-68a2-4078-a52f-1e74d9790bfd)

6 - restart the browser 
7 - login to the client application that requires a 2FA (acr_values=2 for instance)
8 - the user is prompted for a TOTP (the password is not required as we are in a "remember me" session.
9 - **first bad behaviour** : when the user enter the TOTP, an error is displayed

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1fc26ad-26c5-4464-8a5d-7b18db5da295)
10 - **second bad behaviour** :  on the second attempt, the totp is accepted but, on the redirect with the code response, KC sets a new KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY which is now a "session" cookie

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e342c36-c6a1-468a-bb3e-2cc2c6a185fe)
11 - restart the browser 
12 : Login to a client application ==> no keycloak cookie, the user is challenged for a new session

### Anything else?

After the first browser restart, the AUTH_SESSION_ID is absent and the /auth resource can retrieve the persistant KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY cookie but it does not recreate the AUTH_SESSION_ID session cookie when the authenticator display the TOTP form.

When the user submit the first TOTP form, on the /authenticate resource, there is no AUTH_SESSION_ID cookie but this /authenticate resource does not fallback on the KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY persistent cookie.
==> the TOTP error is displayed and a new AUTH_SESSION_ID session cookie is set.
 
After the second TOTP submission, the flow ends and a new KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY cookie (session and no more persistent) is set 

A pull request will be provided as soon as possible to illustrate our investigation and propose a modification for this method :
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/cc4a413db0fc3bf090e381ea4585bbed4561be8e/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationSessionManager.java#L332

issue_title: Configuring log levels for package names with underscores
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When configuring category-specific logging levels for Java classes that use underscores in their package name, the configuration does not take effect.

### Version

26.1.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

You can set different log levels for specific areas in Keycloak

### Actual behavior

Log level configuration is ignored if it is a package containing an underscore character

### How to Reproduce?

- Implement SPI in a Java package that contains an underscore in its name (e.g. `reproducer.not_ok`)
- Add a `org.jboss.logging.Logger` as a static member and call it somewhere to emit log messages, say one on `DEBUG` level and one on `ERROR` level
- Configure [category-specific log levels](https://www.keycloak.org/server/logging#_configuring_category_specific_log_levels). It doesn't matter whether it is by environment variable `KC_LOG_LEVEL` or by command line argument `--log-level="error,reproducer.not_ok:debug"`
- Observe logging output
    - KC 26.0.8: `ERROR` and `DEBUG` level messages are logged as expected
    - KC 26.1.3: Only `ERROR` level message is logged

Reproducer: https://github.com/mandritschke/reproduce-log-level-issue

### Anything else?

There shouldn't be any problem in using the underscore in a package name since the underscore is an acceptable Java letter as per JLS. Some domain names may contain hyphens which are by convention usually converted to underscores in a package name. This issue breaks log level configuration at that domain level.

A workaround would be to go up or down the package name hierarchy.

issue_title: Config expression may use the wrong value
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

This is more of an internal issue, than a user facing one as we don't document how to use arbitrary smallrye expressions - just env references.

In our application.properties we currently have:

```
quarkus.datasource.metrics.enabled=${quarkus.micrometer.enabled:false}
```

quarkus.datasource.metrics.enabled is not directly mapped to by a PropertyMapper, but quarkus.micrometer.enabled is. The only reason it nearly currently works is that the we have logic in our config sources that create placeholders for mapped quarkus properties. So when using --metrics-enabled=true, we create an entry in the config args config source quarkus.micrometer.enabled=true. However this is only supposed to be a fake entry for exposing that quarkus property name, not its value as it's mapped directly to the raw value and not transformed. 

If you ask the configuration directly for quarkus.micrometer.enabled, it will go through the property mapping interceptor to fully resolve the value. If you instead use quarkus.micrometer.enabled in an expression, it will only have access to the raw value from the fake entry because the expression interceptor is below the expression mapping interceptor.

### Version

26

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Nested referenced to mapped properties should be evaluated consistently.

### Actual behavior

Nested references are evaluated potentially incorrectly.

### How to Reproduce?

See the description.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Save Button Not Enabled When Switching OTP Type from "Time Based" to "Counter Based"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In OTP Policy settings(Authentication>Policies), there are two radio buttons to select the OTP Type: "Time Based" and "Counter Based". The default selection is "Time Based". However, when switching to "Counter Based", the Save button does not get enabled, preventing from saving the new configuration

### Version

### Actual behavior

The Save button remains disabled even after switching from "Time Based" to "Counter Based", making it impossible to save the new OTP configuration.
Users need to modify another field in the OTP Policy settings to enable the Save button.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4922bc7f-b7b6-4fd9-bb37-243f2ee220a7)

### How to Reproduce?

- Log in to the Keycloak Admin Console.
- Navigate to Authentication > OTP Policy.
- Locate the OTP Type section, which has two radio buttons: Time Based and Counter Based.
- Switch from "Time Based" (default) to "Counter Based".
- Observe that the Save button remains disabled.

### Anything else?

issue_title: Organization - Identity-First Flow automatic redirect only works with domain in login name
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When using Identity-First Flow with organizations with automatic redirect. The redirect never happens when specifying username without the domain even if the email in the profile has the domain matched and the user is already assigned to the organization but not linked with the corresponding identity provider.

### Version

latest / 26.1.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I would expect the system to fallback to the domain from the email in the profile if no domain can be extracted from login name (when using the username that do not have a domain).

### Actual behavior

When specifying the username in the Identity-First flow the Authenticator (OrganizationAuthenticator) is already fetching the user. During the resolveOrganization check there is already a fallback to the email address specified in the user profile (Organizations.resolveOrganization). 

But when processing in the authenticator the redirect is never executed (OrganizationAuthenticator.tryRedirectBroker) because domain is null (see OrganizationAuthenticator.redirect null check) as it cannot be extracted from the login username. 

### How to Reproduce?

Configure an organization "test" with a linked Identity Provider with option  "Redirect when email domain matches" enabled listing on domain "test.com".

Additionally there is a user with the username testuser and the email testuser@test.com and manually linked to the test organization. 

When activating login with email the user is automatically redirected to the external identity provider after specifying "testuser@test.com". 

When specifying the username "testuser" as the username the user is not redirected to the external identity provider.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OIDC Backchannel Logout does not honour pairwise subject identifier
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

The logout token in Backchannel logout does not contain the hashed UUID for clients with Pairwise Subject Identifiers.

[KEYCLOAK-2940](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-2940) introduced OIDC Backchannel logout.

[KEYCLOAK-6659](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-6659) enabled OIDC Pairwise Subject Identifiers.

See also https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-18725.
There has also been an long time ago closed issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/11735

### Version

26.1.x ( available since introduction of pairwise subject identifier hashing)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When the client receives a backchannel logout there is a JWT containing a subject of the user which sessions should be ended.
If the client received a Token with a hashed subject (Pairwise Subject Identifier Hashing) it only knows the user by this ID. 
Therefore, the logout needs to also logout by the hashed Identifier.

### Actual behavior

The logout Token does not know about Pairwise Subject Identifier Hashing.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a client
2. Add a Pairwise Subject Identifier protocol mapper
3. Configure backchannel logout using some test service for receiving logout requests
4. Compare subject ids of ID token and logout token

### Anything else?

There was PR https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8386 which was rejected due the missing tests.

Add Pairwise Subject Identifier Mapper to Logout tokens

Closes #37839

issue_title: ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator fails to set CONFIGURE_TOTP required action for LDAP read-only users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When LDAP user federation is configured in read-only mode it is not possible to set required actions for users from LDAP. 
Currently, `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` attempts to set the `CONFIGURE_TOTP` required action when a user does not have OTP credentials configured. However, this operation fails with a `org.keycloak.storage.ReadOnlyException`.
Evaluate whether it is possible to add the required action to the authentication session instead of directly to the user, as done in `OTPFormAuthenticator`.


### Version

26.1.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` should trigger the `CONFIGURE_TOTP` required action for any user without OTP credentials, even if the user is from an LDAP federation in read-only mode.

### Actual behavior

The required action `CONFIGURE_TOTP` is not triggered by `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator` if the user belongs to an LDAP user federation configured in read-only mode.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Setup ldap user federation in read-only mode.
2. Configure custom auth flow with `ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator`.
3. Try to login with an ldap user without otp credential configured.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Set CONFIGURE_OTP via auth session in ConditionalOtpFormAuthenticator

Closes #37869

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Make JGroups with TLS startup more robust
issue_body:
### Description

With zero-configuration encryption, the certificates are rotated regularly. During this procedure, a node may join the cluster to obtain the old certificate and be unable to join the cluster.

For testing purposes, the certificates are rotated every 5 seconds, and it leaks the short rotation period to the remaining tests. I suspect it is causing failures in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/37798

I cannot claim it fixes all the instability in `Clustering IT`, as those flaky tests happened before this feature was introduced, but it seems to improve it: https://github.com/pruivo/keycloak/actions/runs/13701043754.
commit_message:
Make JGroups with TLS startup more robust

Fixes #37887

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>

issue_title: Unwanted placeholder texts in user profile fields
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If no placeholder text is specified for user profile fields, the admin UI defaults to trying to resolve a text with the name of the attribute as a key. I think this is a bug, since this floods the UI with placeholder texts that are most often either a) the name of the attribute (which is useless to the end user) or b) the same as the label which already precedes the field in the UI.

Even worse; we have an attribute named "fullName" with no placeholder text defined (we don't want any). Now, placeholder text appears like this:

![Unwanted placeholder text](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f51ab8a0-1e94-4040-aff2-2e7332c55b05)

Since "fullName" key is defined as `{{givenName}} {{familyName}}` in message bundles.

I believe this occurs after fix for #31111, specifically [this commit](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/c8d64f289105c979a99fcbf7fd4b64b976be2805).

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If no placeholder text is defined, no placeholder text should be displayed. This is in line with most UX recommendations, e.g. https://uxmovement.com/forms/stop-misusing-placeholder-text-on-textfields/

### Actual behavior

Always displays placeholder text containing either attribute name or a matching message from message bundles.

### How to Reproduce?

Add managed attribute called "fullName" to see the effect.

### Anything else?

The issue #31111 does not seem to intent on introducing this behaviour, as it only relates to `inputOptionLabelsI18nPrefix`
commit_message:
removed fallback to attribute name on placeholder (#38005)

fixes: #37911

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

### How to Reproduce?

Send a policy evaluation request for a UMA resource by setting only the resource name and not the ID.

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I have a custom extension that has one configuration property of type `MAP_TYPE` that sets a default value. Since the new admin UI console the default value has been ignored for this property type and just comes up null. 

### Version

v26.1.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The default property would be used and appear in the UI.

### Actual behavior

#20235

👋 Hello,

> The given key is not a valid key per specification, future migration might fail

26.1.3

- OjPxlVS-TROZkEmkuGoqEw
- RaQXxaH_SGamVvd-6CBB2w

Create a LDAP based user federation via web admin console or nodejs admin client.

issue_title: User session limit exceeded for both realm and client removes the wrong session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When enabling User Session Count Limiter the wrong session is removed if the behavior is set to "Terminate oldest session" and the limit is exceeded for both realm and client at the same time. The oldest session is removed regardless of client.

When session limit for realm is set to 3 and client session limit is set to 1 and a user has 2 active sessions with client-1 and client-2, when the user then creates a new session with client-2 the old session for client-2 is removed regardless of which session is the oldest.

If the a user has 3 active sessions; client-1, client-2 and client-3, and the user creates a new session the oldest session is removed regardless of client. I.e. the user can end up with 3 active sessions; client-1, client-1 and client-3.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If both realm user session limit and client user session limit exceeds at the same time the oldest session of the same client should be removed first.

### Actual behavior

The oldest user session is removed regardless of client.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a flow with a required "User session count lmiter" step
2. Configure the step to have the "Terminate oldest session" behavior and realm session limit 3 and client session limit 1
3. Create client-1, client-2 & client-3 and configure them to use the authentication flow from above
4. Login on client-1
5. Login on client-2
6. Observe that the user has 2 active sessions
7. Login on client-2
8. Observe that the user has 2 active session (client-1 and client-2)
9. Login on client-3
10. Observe that the user has 3 active session (client-1, client-2 and client-3)
11. Login on client-3
12. Observe that the user has 3 active session (client-2, client-3 and client-3)

### Anything else?

_No response_

BUGFIX: session limit exceeded for both client & realm

This commit fixes a bug the wrong user session is removed if the user session limit
for realm and client is exceeded at the same time.

Closes #38016

issue_title: User created with undefined locale except when they explicitely select their language
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Hello Keycloak team,  

I’d like to highlight an important issue that may require your attention:  

When users create an account in Keycloak, they are not assigned a language unless they explicitly select one from the dropdown on the login page.  

As a result, confirmation and password reset emails are sent in the realm’s default language.  

### Version

Likely all versions, certainly the latest.

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Typically, an application provides the `ui_locales` query parameter when redirecting to the authorization URL.  

If `ui_locales` is not explicitly provided, Keycloak checks the `Accept-Language` header and attempts to match it with an enabled language in the realm. If no match is found, the page is rendered in the realm’s default locale.  

The locale determined through this process should be assigned to the user upon account creation.  

### Actual behavior

Unless the user explicitly selects a language from the dropdown, the account is created without a defined locale, causing emails to be sent in the realm's default language.  

This happens almost every time, as users have no reason to use the language selector when the login page is already displayed in their preferred language—either because the client application set it via `ui_locales` or Keycloak detected it based on the browser's language preferences.  

### How to Reproduce?

You may have your own way of running Keycloak, but here's how to do it using the Keycloakify tooling:  

```bash
git clone https://github.com/keycloakify/keycloakify-starter
cd keycloakify-starter
yarn
npx keycloakify start-keycloak
# Select any Keycloak version
```


1. Enable the default theme (this issue does not occur with the Keycloakify theme, as it has been fixed there).  
    Navigate to [https://localhost:8080](https://localhost:8080) and log in with `admin:admin`.  
3. Select **"myrealm"**.  
4. In the **Themes** tab, enable the default login theme.  
5. Open [https://my-theme.keycloakify.dev](https://my-theme.keycloakify.dev).  
6. Append `&ui_locales=es` to the URL and reload the page.  
7. Create an account.  
8. Go back to the **Admin Console** and check the newly created user—the language has not been set.  

### Anything else?

I fixed this in Keycloakify by making a fetch request to login-action/registrations?kc_locale=xx, but this shouldn’t be necessary.

Wishing you all the best!
commit_message:
Add locale attribute to the registration context
Closes #38029

issue_title: Issue in clearing offline sessions internally using ClearExpiredUserSessions Scheduled task
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

When "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" realm attribute is set with some value > 0, eg: 180 and  "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled": false, Then the refresh_token got for the client/user has no expiration for offline token, which is the expected behaviour based on "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled".

Keycloak internally runs a scheduled task for every 15 minutes to cleanup the expired offline sessions. where it cleans the offline sessions only based on "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout", which makes the refresh_token invalid.

Note: In UI we doesn't have support to add "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" but it can be set via update realm API.

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

when the "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" realm attribute is set to **false** the refresh_token shouldn't be cleared even when the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" attribute has value.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak internally clears the offline session when "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" has value and expiry is calculated with the value and current time even the "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" is false.

### How to Reproduce?

- Using update realm endpoint update the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout" to greater than 0 (Eg: 120) and "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled" to false.
- Get an refresh_token using token endpoint and verify the field "refresh_expires_in" has value 0.
- Verify the refresh_token using offline token exchange and it should able to get the new access_token.
- Wait for some time at least for 15 to 30 minutes, where scheduled task to run internally for every 15minutes to clean the offline session based on the "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout".
- Then verify the same refresh_token using offline token exchange, it'll throw error as "invalid_grant", "session doesn't have required client".

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Login with admin-cli not possible with password starting with "@@"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

The login via the Admin CLI of Keycloak fails for a user whose password begins with two '@' characters. However, logging in through the UI and API works as expected. This issue occurs in versions 25.0.6 and 26.1.3, but not in version 24.0.5.

### Version

25.0.6

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user should be able to log in successfully using the Admin CLI, just as they can through the UI and API.

### Actual behavior

When attempting to log in via the Admin CLI with a password that starts with two '@' characters, the following error message is returned: "Invalid user credentials [invalid_grant]".

### How to Reproduce?

Create a user in Keycloak.
Set the user's password to begin with two '@' characters (e.g., @@password123).
Attempt to log in using the Admin CLI with the created user.
Observe the error message: "Invalid user credentials [invalid_grant]".

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Worse UX with new realm selector
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The newly introduced realm selector has some usability concerns, and honestly doesn't look very polished either.

Some issues I've identified:

* Placement on the top menu is not ideal at least not for my usual flows; my usual flow is to focus on the left hand menu, and with the old placement in the left menu it was quicker to glance at what realm was active, and change the realm is needed. Now I need to focus both my eyes and the mouse on a different place in the UI
* There's a lot of noise as there is now 3 elements (label + dropdown + create realm), this makes it look rather unpolished
* It's very close to and distracts from the Keycloak logo, which visually isn't very pleasing
* With less than 10 realms the drop-down has "Current realm", "Recently used", and the rest. This means I need to look two places when switching to a realm. First looking in recently used, then it all the rest. I don't think there should be recently used at all when there is 10 realms or less, just list the current realm, then the rest
* The dropdown has entries that are multi lines if a realm has display name set. This makes the dropdown awkward as the size of the elements differ. I would rather just show the display name if there is one, otherwise realm name. Alternatively some way of showing both on a single line would be okay
* The dropdown is very narrow if there is no realms with a long display name or realm name; would be good to have a minimum-width set
* Create realm isn't something that is used super frequently; while now it takes up pretty much the most valuable real estate on the UI

### Version

main

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Better UX

### Actual behavior

Worse UX

### How to Reproduce?

Open the admin UI ;)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Profile Custom Attribute Group: Click on attribute group changes URL, breaking the navigation in AdminUI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

There seems to be a problem with the user-profile attribute-groups navigation, breaking the browser-back feature, leading the user to an undefined state. Reloading the AdminUI solves the issue.



### Version

26.1.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A click on the group scrolls the group in view. Triggering a browser-back action is going to the previous viewed page/tab or attribute-group. 

### Actual behavior

 If a user is on a user-profile page and clicks on a custom attribute-group (screenshot 1; "Details") the URL is changing (see screenshot 2) and the browser hops to the correct group. If a user is now trying to go back, using the browser-back button, the navigation breaks, leading the user to an invalid page (screenshot 3). From there almost every interaction leads to further errors (screenshot 4). Refreshing the page helps but also sets the user back to the initial page.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61661cf5-7c48-4780-a693-261c4d965937)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8bb0091-7a7c-4df3-9342-1638bba9520b)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c18d63dc-e6e9-4e12-963d-c48c5dcbcc61)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f6096d3-b361-496e-84c5-cb6fa979847d)

### How to Reproduce?

- Install Keycloak
- Add Realm
- Add custom profile fields with a custom attribute group
- Go to a realm user
- Click on the non-default attribute group (right hand of attributes)
- Trigger a browser-back action
- Try to navigate away from the page

### Anything else?

The url-change behavior is the same for AdminUI and AccountUI but only the admin-ui navigation is affected by this. In account-ui everything works as expected.
